library_preparation_preflight.summary: durian-io is a split Guava/Durian IO+hash artifact whose POM declares durian-core and durian-collect as runtime dependencies; Gradle's normal generated scaffold will not put those runtime-scoped dependencies on the test compile classpath, but meaningful IO APIs expose/use collect/core types such as ImmutableList, TreeTraverser, Splitter, and primitive/base helpers.

deterministic_setup.reason: durian-io classes directly depend on com.diffplug.common.base, primitives, math, and annotations from durian-core; adding it explicitly makes those normal Durian helper types available to generated tests at compile time.

common durian-io APIs expose durian-collect types, for example CharSource.readLines returns ImmutableList and Files.fileTreeTraverser returns TreeTraverser; without durian-collect on the test compile classpath, meaningful API calls can fail to compile.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment/system property setup was found. Generated tests should use local temporary files/directories and in-memory streams/readers/writers for IO coverage rather than external services.
risks: The artifact's POM uses runtime scope for durian-core and durian-collect, so runtime execution may work with only durian-io, but Java compilation of tests that call APIs exposing those types can still fail unless the dependencies are added explicitly.

Upstream tests include broader Durian/Guava-style test helpers, but those are not required for generated reachability coverage and should not be added unless local Gradle verification proves they are needed.
